name: Bodyweight Calf Raise
resistance_profile: Tension peaks at the bottom with heels dropped below the step and the calves in a deep stretch — strong lengthened loading.
cues: Ball of the foot on a step
Full stretch down, tall squeeze up
execution_steps: Stand with the balls of both feet on the edge of a step or plate, heels hanging off
Hold a rail or wall for balance and stand tall
Lower your heels below the step until you feel a deep stretch in the calves
Drive up onto your toes as high as possible and squeeze at the top
Pause briefly, then lower slowly under control
good_when: Training at home or with no equipment
A complete beginner learning the calf-raise pattern
Adding high-rep calf volume as a finisher
bad_when: Bodyweight no longer challenges you within your rep range (needs added load)
You want to isolate the soleus (use a seated calf raise instead)
